summary: Full IFC2X3 runtime bundle for building-centric models. Use it for classic project/site/building/storey decomposition, product containment, and roof-to-slab aggregate traversal. In this schema, IfcRoof exposes ShapeType rather than the later PredefinedType field.
cautions: IfcRoof uses ShapeType in IFC2X3_TC1; do not look for IfcRoof.PredefinedType here.
IfcRoof and IfcSlab both sit under IfcBuildingElement in this schema, not the later IfcBuiltElement family.
A direct roof hide may fail if the visible roof geometry is carried by related slabs rather than the IfcRoof node itself.
Use DB node ids for graph actions and GlobalId values for viewer element actions.
queryHabits: Use id(n) AS node_id when you want graph seeds or graph explanations.
Use GlobalId AS global_id when you want hide, show, or select actions in the viewer.
Start with small local queries and add LIMIT unless the task clearly needs a full scan.
Inspect aggregate, containment, type, property, and material relations before trusting an enum to explain an element's role.
Treat IfcRoof as a semantic starting point and inspect related slabs before trying viewer element actions.

IfcProject.summary: Semantic root for the IFC model. Good default seed when the user wants to orient the graph around the whole project.
queryPatterns: MATCH (p:IfcProject) RETURN id(p) AS node_id LIMIT 1

IfcBuildingStorey.summary: Spatial structure node used for storey membership and containment explanations.
queryPatterns: MATCH (s:IfcBuildingStorey) RETURN id(s) AS node_id LIMIT 8

IfcRoof.summary: Semantic roof product. Often an aggregate parent rather than the node that carries visible roof geometry.
queryPatterns: MATCH (r:IfcRoof) RETURN id(r) AS node_id LIMIT 8
MATCH (:IfcRoof)<--(:IfcRelAggregates)-->(slab:IfcSlab) RETURN DISTINCT id(slab) AS node_id LIMIT 16

IfcSlab.summary: General slab product. Often carries visible roof or floor geometry and is usually the better target for viewer actions.
queryPatterns: MATCH (slab:IfcSlab) RETURN id(slab) AS node_id LIMIT 8
MATCH (:IfcRoof)<--(:IfcRelAggregates)-->(slab:IfcSlab) RETURN DISTINCT slab.GlobalId AS global_id LIMIT 32

IfcRelAggregates.summary: Main decomposition/aggregate relation. Use it for project/site/building/storey hierarchy and for roof-to-slab traversal.
queryPatterns: MATCH (:IfcRoof)<--(:IfcRelAggregates)-->(slab:IfcSlab) RETURN DISTINCT id(slab) AS node_id LIMIT 16

IfcRelContainedInSpatialStructure.summary: Containment relation between products and spatial structure such as storeys and buildings.
queryPatterns: MATCH (e:IfcWall)<--(:IfcRelContainedInSpatialStructure)-->(s:IfcBuildingStorey) RETURN id(e) AS node_id, id(s) AS storey_node_id LIMIT 16

IfcRelDefinesByType.summary: Type attachment relation. Useful when the product label is too weak to explain the element's role cleanly.

IfcRelDefinesByProperties.summary: Property-set attachment relation. Use it when type or enum values are too opaque to answer in human terms.

IfcRelAssociatesMaterial.summary: Material attachment relation. Useful for questions about what a wall or slab is made from.

projectSeed: MATCH (p:IfcProject) RETURN id(p) AS node_id LIMIT 1
wallNeighborhood: MATCH (w:IfcWall) RETURN id(w) AS node_id LIMIT 8
roofToSlabsGraph: MATCH (:IfcRoof)<--(:IfcRelAggregates)-->(slab:IfcSlab) RETURN DISTINCT id(slab) AS node_id LIMIT 16
roofToSlabsViewer: MATCH (:IfcRoof)<--(:IfcRelAggregates)-->(slab:IfcSlab) RETURN DISTINCT slab.GlobalId AS global_id LIMIT 32
